day1_insight: There's been no measurable rain lately, so there's no runoff driving contamination, and strong summer sunlight is helping disinfect the water. The model's main signals right now: high tide level is lowering risk — a high tide can mobilize bacteria held in wet sand and push the waterline toward outfalls; strong sunlight is lowering risk by inactivating bacteria with UV; and the lack of recent rain is keeping runoff-driven contamination low. The last lab sample (10 MPN/100mL) is now 22 days old — beaches are sampled about weekly, so a reading this stale carries little weight, and this estimate leans on current conditions.
